free_text: Neutron and fragment yields in proton-induced fission
of U-238 at intermediate energies
The yields of
neutron-rich Ni, Cu, Zn, Ga and Ge-isotopes were
measured in 25-MeV proton induced fission of U-238.
A 100 mug/cm**2 layer of uranium-238 evaporated on
60 mug/cm**2 thick Al/2-O/3 backing, was used.
A new version of the theoretical model which is
a combination of a model for calculating of fission
product yields and a time-dependent statistical model
for fission process with inclusion of dynamical
effects.
The measurements were carried out using
the high efficiency neutron detection system (HENDES)
facility at the accelerator laboratory, university of
Jyvaskyla
Seven position-sensitive neutron detector
(PSND), Two Large Position-Sensitive Avalanche
counters (PSAC) and one micro-channel plate (MCP)
start detector.
PSAC, Having a time resolution of better than 400-PS,
position resolution below 1-mm and diameter of 2450-mm,
were tuned to be insensitive to alpha-particles.
The MCP, with 100 mug/cm**2 thick gold plated mylar
converted foil and intrinsic time resolution of 100-PS,
was placed in front of one of the PSAC detectors.
Both PSAC and mcp Detector were Placed Inside a
spherical stainless steel reaction chamber with a
diameter of 80-cm and wall a thickness of 2-mm.
Each of the seven PSND, surrounding the chamber
consisted of a 100-cm long quartz tube with a diameter
of 6-cm filled with 2.31 of NE-213 liquid scintillator.
Data tables for subentries 2 and 4 were
received from D.Vakhtin
Last checking has been done.
SD: Correction in Subent 004.

- By authors.  To extract pre- and post-scission
neutron multiplicities, a multiple-source procedure was
used. Neutrons were assumed to be emitted
isotopically in the corresponding rest-frames of three
moving sources:  compound nucleus and two fully
accelerated fission fragment.
Table 3 from Nucl.Inst.Meth.,A463(2001)653
